channel.title: Tysons Times
channel.description: Tysons Times is an independent local newspaper for Tysons, Virginia, covering civic life, schools, business, dining, events, transportation, and neighborhood change across Northern Fairfax County.

item.title: Tysons' Next Chapter
item.description: Growth around the Silver Line, school traffic, storefront change, and neighborhood preservation move to the front page.
item.author: Tysons Times Staff

item.title: Route 7 Remains the Main Thread
item.description: The corridor ties together errands, restaurants, offices, schools, Metro stations, and the daily questions of walkability.
item.author: Civic Desk

item.title: Tysons Growth Keeps Neighbors Watching
item.description: Development around the Beltway still shapes commutes, restaurant traffic, housing pressure, and local business decisions.
item.author: Business Desk

item.title: Public Meetings Need Better Memory
item.description: Votes, filings, resident questions, and follow-up promises deserve a record that survives beyond the meeting room.
item.author: Tysons Times Staff

item.title: Schools, Fields, Libraries, and Friday Nights
item.description: A local paper has to cover the places where families actually spend their week.
item.author: Schools Desk

item.title: A Weekend Calendar Built for Residents
item.description: The Times calendar template is designed for library talks, school stages, fundraisers, markets, concerts, and civic deadlines.
item.author: Calendar Desk

item.title: Small Business Notebook Starts on the Ground Floor
item.description: Restaurant openings, office moves, retail changes, and independent businesses belong on the local front page.
item.author: Business Desk

item.title: Parks, Trails, and Stream Valleys
item.description: Green space is part of Tysons' identity, from pocket parks to trails and wooded edges around the urban core.
item.author: Features Desk

item.title: Local History Should Stay on the Page
item.description: Historic places, family archives, and neighborhood memory give Tysons reporting a longer timeline.
item.author: History Desk

item.title: Commute Notes Need Plain English
item.description: Readers need clear updates on lane shifts, bus connections, trail access, Metro links, and construction timing.
item.author: Transportation Desk

item.title: Youth Sports Roundup Finds the Names
item.description: Local teams, coaches, volunteers, and athletes deserve a record beyond the final score.
item.author: Sports Desk

item.title: Letters Should Be Local, Signed, and Clear
item.description: The opinion page will make space for reader arguments without losing accountability.
item.author: Opinion Editor
